description_long: This object depicts a small residential complex consisting of four modern houses arranged in two parallel rows on a landscaped, sloped plot. Each house features a flat roof with rooftop terraces, large windows, and a combination of white and wood-toned exterior finishes. The houses are separated by low retaining walls and connected by paved walkways and steps that follow the terrain's incline. The site includes green lawn areas, raised platforms, and a perimeter fence, with the terrain modeled to show a significant elevation change. The entryways are accessible via wooden walkways and steps, and each house appears to have a private outdoor space. The overall design emphasizes geometric forms, open terraces, and integration with the sloped landscape.
description: A modern residential complex of four flat-roofed houses with rooftop terraces, large windows, and wood-accented facades, arranged on a landscaped, sloped plot with walkways and retaining walls.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entry walkways, terraces, and the arrangement of all four houses with visible steps and landscaping.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the sloped terrain, retaining walls, and the staggered positioning of the houses along the incline.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back facades of the houses, the extent of the sloped plot, and the minimal window openings on this side.
description_view_3: Angled view from the opposite side, showing the entry paths, terraces, and the relationship between the houses and the landscape.
attribute_window_arrangement: symmetrical rows with large rectangular windows
attribute_entryway_design: Main entryways feature steps, wooden walkways, and minimal overhangs, with simple modern doors and no prominent decorative elements.
